title: Market Brief — Friday, June 12, 2026
summary: GPU supply is loose across the board. Util-avail stands at 31% for A100, 41% for H100, 60% for H200, and 54% for B200, with H200 firmly in oversupply territory after a staggering 58pp utilization collapse in 30 days. The biggest daily price move was A100 spot jumping 19.8% to $1.05/hr, a dead-cat bounce on the oldest iron in the rack while everything newer bleeds demand.
paragraphs: GPU supply is loose across the board. Util-avail stands at 31% for A100, 41% for H100, 60% for H200, and 54% for B200, with H200 firmly in oversupply territory after a staggering 58pp utilization collapse in 30 days. The biggest daily price move was A100 spot jumping 19.8% to $1.05/hr, a dead-cat bounce on the oldest iron in the rack while everything newer bleeds demand.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $8.65/M tokens and $8.83/M on H200, meaning DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M output and Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M are selling at losses exceeding $8/M tokens, pure cash burn to hold market share. OpenAI's o3 Pro at $80/M output prints money at any tier. Grades: OpenAI Strong, Anthropic Neutral, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash, Meta Burning Cash.

B200 at $4.38/hr spot with 77% of NeoCloud offerings sitting idle is overvalued compute begging for a repricing below $4. The undervalued trade is routing Llama 70B inference to A100s at $1.05/hr where TCO-adjusted cost is $6.61/M and premium-tier margins still clear 88%.
bullets: 60% util-avail on H200 signals oversupply after a 58 percentage point utilization drop in 30 days.
31% util-avail on A100 makes it the tightest GPU class despite being the oldest hardware.
$80/M output for OpenAI o3 Pro is 235x more expensive than DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.34/M.
21.78 on the LLMflation Index means frontier token prices have fallen roughly 78% since GPT-4 launch.
$3.20/M TCO-adjusted cost on B200 gives it the only positive margin at the medium pricing tier at negative 7%.
77% of advertised B200 NeoCloud offerings are unfilled, the highest vacancy rate across all GPU classes.
